questions: Should active intents have a TTL/expiry? If the recipient never replies, should the intent auto-expire after N hours/days? If so, what timeout?

If the admin initiates a second payment for the same recipient in the same channel while one is already active, should it overwrite, reject, or allow parallel intents? The current plan uses a unique index that prevents duplicates per (guild, channel, recipient) for active intents.

For the agent classification of wallet/confirmation replies: should this use the same Anthropic API key and model as the admin chat agent, or a lighter/cheaper model? The classification prompt is very short and deterministic.

The SQL schema file sql/admin_payment_intents.sql needs to be applied to the Supabase database. Is there a deployment step that runs these SQL files, or should the plan include instructions for manual application?

success_criteria.criterion: admin_payment_intents table exists in sql/admin_payment_intents.sql with status state machine, message cursor fields, and proper indexes
db_handler has CRUD methods for admin_payment_intents: create, get by ID, get active for recipient (identity-gated lookup), list active, update
PaymentService.request_payment accepts optional amount_token parameter and creates payment with direct SOL amount when provided (amount_usd=None, token_price_usd=None)
initiate_payment tool appears in TOOLS list, ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS set, and the assertion at tools.py:916 passes
initiate_payment accepts amount_sol (not amount_usd) and does NOT accept wallet_address
When recipient has wallet on file: initiate_payment creates persisted intent with status=awaiting_test and starts test payment with producer='admin_chat'
When no wallet on file: initiate_payment creates persisted intent with status=awaiting_wallet, sends channel ping, stores prompt_message_id
initiate_payment fails closed when called from DM (no guild_id/source_channel_id)
initiate_payment fails closed on invalid recipient_user_id or amount_sol <= 0
Duplicate active intent for same (guild, channel, recipient) is prevented and returns existing intent info
Reply interceptor fires before _is_directed_at_bot and _can_user_message_bot gates in on_message
Intent matching is deterministic: (guild_id, channel_id, author_id) lookup returns single active intent before any agent classification
Agent classification only runs after single candidate intent is identified, categorizing reply as wallet_provided/positive_confirmation/declined/ambiguous/suspicious
Ambiguous, suspicious, or out-of-ordinary reply patterns fail closed and tag admin for review
Valid wallet reply triggers upsert_wallet, updates intent status+cursor, starts test payment
handle_payment_result for test-confirmed creates final payment with amount_token (direct SOL, no USD conversion) and posts both PaymentConfirmView button and free-text confirmation prompt
handle_payment_result for test-failed updates intent to failed and does NOT create final payment
Free-text positive confirmation calls confirm_payment with confirmed_by='free_text' and updates intent
PaymentConfirmView button confirmation also works as secondary path (existing button handler, no changes needed)
producer_ref includes timestamp to satisfy unique index on (producer, producer_ref, is_test)
source_channel_id injected in agent.py using key that does not conflict with existing tools
Startup reconciliation in cog_load queries active intents, scans channel history from last_scanned_message_id, processes missed replies
Intent status and message cursors are persisted in DB (survives restart)
All existing tests pass: test_scheduler.py, test_social_route_tools.py, full suite
System prompt documents initiate_payment with amount_sol and guild-channel requirement

New test file covers: both wallet paths, validation, duplicate prevention, wallet reply handling, agent classification, terminal callbacks, confirmation, concurrent intents, startup reconciliation, amount_token parameter
Multiple concurrent intents in same channel for different recipients handled correctly
execute_initiate_payment and _start_admin_payment_flow are each under ~80 lines
handle_payment_result follows GrantsCog structure: guard then branch is_test then branch status
_check_pending_payment_reply has minimal overhead for non-matching messages (single DB query on indexed columns)
Race-condition guard prevents double-processing of same intent reply
Agent classification prompt is concise and deterministic with clear category definitions
Manual smoke test: admin says 'pay @user 5 SOL' in channel, bot pings user, user replies wallet, test queues, confirmation button appears, user replies 'yes', payout executes

assumptions: Producer name is 'admin_chat' — PaymentCog._candidate_producer_cog_names('admin_chat') produces ['AdminChatCog', ...] auto-matching the cog with zero PaymentCog changes.
Admin must initiate from a guild channel, not DM. Admin DMs have guild_id=None (AdminChatCog skips guild resolution for admins) and no channel to ping recipient in.
The wallet-reply interceptor fires before _is_directed_at_bot() and _can_user_message_bot(). Recipient does not need can_message_bot permission.
Agent classification uses the same Anthropic client already available on AdminChatAgent, with a short classification-specific prompt (not a full chat turn).

The unique index on (guild_id, channel_id, recipient_user_id) for active intents means only one active intent per recipient per channel. A second initiate_payment for the same user in the same channel returns the existing intent.
handle_payment_result posts producer-specific messages to the notify channel in addition to PaymentCog's generic terminal notification (same pattern as GrantsCog).
No maximum payment amount guardrail — admin is trusted.
The DB schema file sql/admin_payment_intents.sql will be applied to Supabase via the same deployment process as sql/payments.sql (manual or automated).

For startup recovery, channel.history() scan is limited to a reasonable number of messages (e.g., 200) to avoid excessive API calls. Older unresolved intents should be flagged for admin review rather than scanning indefinitely.
